title: Isoplexis isabelliana
taxonomy: Isoplexis isabelliana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, although some sources may group it in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a member of the genus Isoplexis and is native to the Canary Islands. This plant has undergone reclassification, and some sources may refer to it as a synonym of Isoplexis canariensis or as a part of the genus Veronica. However, the most widely accepted classification places it in the family Scrophulariaceae. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of this plant.
uses: Isoplexis isabelliana, also known as the Madeira fuchsia, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, Isoplexis isabelliana is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. It is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may help in wound healing and preventing infections.
